critic_raw:
The Counterfactual Generative Networks paper has methodological issues around its evaluation and causal claims.

1. The paper uses IS and FID to evaluate sample quality and diversity. Both metrics depend on the Inception network's feature space, which was trained on ImageNet. For counterfactual image generation tasks where the target distribution differs significantly from ImageNet (e.g., medical images or specialized domains), IS and FID are poor proxies for actual sample quality and diversity. The evaluation does not include domain-appropriate quality metrics.

2. The counterfactual samples are conditioned on 'counterfactual contexts sampled from rich prior knowledge sources.' The nature and quality of these prior knowledge sources is critical — if the prior is itself biased or limited in counterfactual coverage, the generated samples will not represent the true counterfactual distribution. The paper does not include an ablation showing how sample quality degrades as the prior knowledge source becomes less informative.

3. The discriminator evaluates authenticity against counterfactual conditions. This creates a training dynamic where the discriminator and generator are jointly optimized — standard GAN instability problems (mode collapse, gradient vanishing) apply, but the paper does not report training stability analysis or provide convergence diagnostics. High FID/IS at evaluation time does not exclude the possibility that the model achieved these scores via mode collapse on a small subset of the counterfactual distribution.

4. The claimed improvement in diversity is evaluated using aggregate metrics. A GAN can score well on FID while generating diverse samples only within a narrow subspace of the true distribution. The paper does not report precision/recall decomposition (e.g., Kynkaanniemi et al. 2019) that would distinguish diversity from fidelity and reveal whether the counterfactual generation is achieving genuine distributional coverage.
all_issues_raised: IS and FID use Inception features trained on ImageNet; metrics are invalid proxies for counterfactual generation quality in non-ImageNet domains
Prior knowledge source quality is not ablated; biased or limited priors directly degrade counterfactual distribution coverage
GAN training instability and mode collapse are not analyzed; high aggregate metrics are compatible with mode-collapsed generation
Diversity claims are supported by aggregate FID/IS without precision/recall decomposition; genuine distributional coverage is not verified
Prior distribution over counterfactuals is vaguely specified; different implementations would produce different results
FID and IS measure overall distributional quality, not counterfactual validity; model may ignore conditioning
Dual discriminator objective (quality + counterfactual consistency) creates training instability and shortcut risk
Diversity claim via IS does not decompose within-counterfactual from between-counterfactual variation
Counterfactual realism not quantified; some counterfactual configurations may produce geometrically implausible images
IS and FID measure marginal generation quality, not counterfactual fidelity; a model ignoring conditioning would still score well on these metrics
Prior knowledge source validity is not evaluated; incorrect causal priors produce causally incoherent counterfactuals even if visually realistic
Discriminator may fail to enforce conditional structure due to training instability; no diagnostic verifies the discriminator is conditioning on counterfactual context
Diversity improvement conflates aggregate class coverage with within-class diversity; conditional diversity not separated from unconditional aggregate diversity
Counterfactual query specification requires external domain knowledge whose sensitivity is not evaluated; misspecified queries could introduce new biases
